action.reasoning: Observation: The robot oscillates at 8-10m from human. When angle=-0.4 (human to right), the code correctly applies ang_vel=-3.1 and robot approaches. But then it switches to ang_vel=+15 (wrong direction) causing robot to spiral away. The state machine is broken - it enters SPIN state with wrong spin_dir=+1 when angle is negative. Analysis: The fix is to eliminate the state machine entirely and use pure GPS-compass control: ang_vel = clip(K*angle, -20, 20) always. When |angle| > π/2, reduce forward speed but keep moving. The robot CAN navigate around walls with pure GPS control (it was making progress at 8.84→8.50m). Decision: Rewrite predict.py with pure GPS-compass control, no state machine, no committed directions. Simple proportional control. Also write debug_close4.py to verify this works before submitting eval.
